name: Puerto de la Tejera from Miranda del Ebro — circular through the Protected Biotope of the Diapiro de Añana
name_original: Puerto de la Tejera desde Miranda del Ebro — circular por el Biotopo Protegido del Diapiro de Añana
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Miranda De Ebro traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the Ebro River and the Montes Obarenes mountain range. The region offers varied terrain, from serene river paths to challenging climbs through natural parks. Its strategic location near Álava and La Rioja provides access to a wide array of scenic routes, including those with short, steep inclines. The area is characterized by a mix of paved surfaces ideal for road cycling, with elevation gains that cater to different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Miranda De Ebro The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de la Tejera from Miranda del Ebro — circular through the Protected Biotope of the Diapiro de Añana, a 31.8 miles (51.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes through the Protected Biotope of the Diapiro de Añana. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Oña y La Bureba — circular por el Parque natural Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil, a difficult 83.2 miles (133.9 km) path. This route leads through the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, offering significant elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the Río Tirón – Balcón de Castilla loop from Miranda de Ebro, a 32.3 miles (51.9 km) trail leading through the Balcón de Castilla viewpoint, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Miranda De Ebro is defined by the Ebro River, the Montes Obarenes mountain range, and varied terrain with views towards La Rioja. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Miranda De Ebro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 2,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Miranda De Ebro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Miranda de Ebro?
A: There are over 120 road cycling routes around Miranda de Ebro, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 26 easy routes, 68 moderate routes, and 32 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Miranda de Ebro?
A: The terrain around Miranda de Ebro is quite varied, shaped by the Ebro River and the Montes Obarenes mountain range. You'll find mostly paved surfaces, ideal for road cycling, with options ranging from serene river paths to challenging climbs featuring short, steep inclines. The region's geography provides a mix of flat sections and significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Miranda de Ebro offers several routes suitable for families and beginners. There are 26 easy routes available, providing gentler terrain and shorter distances. These routes often follow the Ebro River, offering pleasant, less demanding rides.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Miranda de Ebro provides 32 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058349596/sierra-de-ona-y-la-bureba-circular-por-el-parque-natural-montes-obarenes-san-zadornil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Oña y La Bureba — circular por el Parque natural Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 133.9 km path with significant elevation gain through the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park.

Q: What scenic views or natural features can I see while cycling?
A: Road cycling routes in Miranda de Ebro offer stunning natural scenery. You can enjoy views of the Ebro River, the majestic Montes Obarenes mountain range, and the Protected Biotope of the Diapiro de Añana. Many routes also pass by viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15007866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscapes, including towards La Rioja.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the routes?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical landmarks. The historic Carlos III Bridge, spanning the Ebro River, is a notable feature. You might also encounter sites like the Monastery of Herrera or pass near charming villages and castles. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sajazarra Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5474818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berganzo Village&lt;/a&gt; are points of interest in the wider region.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Miranda de Ebro?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful natural landscapes, and the variety of routes that cater to different abilities, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents through natural parks.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Miranda de Ebro are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058340497/puerto-de-la-tejera-from-miranda-del-ebro-circular-through-the-protected-biotope-of-the-diapiro-de-anana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Tejera from Miranda del Ebro — circular through the Protected Biotope of the Diapiro de Añana&lt;/a&gt;, a 51.2 km route.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Miranda de Ebro?
A: While specific weather conditions vary, the region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for exploring the diverse landscapes without the extreme heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter days of winter.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through natural parks?
A: Yes, several routes traverse or border natural parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058349596/sierra-de-ona-y-la-bureba-circular-por-el-parque-natural-montes-obarenes-san-zadornil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Oña y La Bureba&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, leads through the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058340497/puerto-de-la-tejera-from-miranda-del-ebro-circular-through-the-protected-biotope-of-the-diapiro-de-anana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Tejera from Miranda del Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, passes through the Protected Biotope of the Diapiro de Añana.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views towards La Rioja?
A: Yes, due to Miranda de Ebro's strategic location near the border of La Rioja, many routes offer views towards this famous wine region. The varied terrain often provides elevated points where you can enjoy expansive vistas, including the vineyard-lined roads characteristic of La Rioja.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: The duration of routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15007866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tirón – Balcón de Castilla loop from Miranda de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; (51.9 km) typically takes around 2 hours and 18 minutes, while more challenging routes can take several hours.
tips: Beautiful town, good climb. Spectacular surroundings
Mountain pass that comes from the town of Pobes and that leads to the Diapiro de Añana Protected Biotop. Their cramps reach above 8-9%.

Lake Arreo or Lake Caicedo Yuso is the most important natural lake in the Basque Country. It is located in the south of the province of Álava and a few kilometers from the Ebro river and a few from the province of Burgos.
On January 17 of each year the festival of San Antón is celebrated in the hermitage.
